summary: Large mncharsel decomp/matching PR touching the character select source and related headers/types. Evidence shows new code in src/melee/mn/mncharsel.c, header/type adjustments, 3 reported new matches, and multiple unmatched-item improvements in main/melee/mn/mncharsel. The PR body is minimal and there are no human review comments in the loaded slice, so lessons are mainly inferred from diff patterns and the bot report.

key_files.role: Primary decomp/matching source change; adds includes, local struct CSSCursorData, many mnCharSel functions/logic, animation calls, switch logic, PAD_STACK, and a tightly scoped dont_inline pragma.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: includes added for trigf, controller, dobj, mobj, gobjgxlink, random, gm_1601, lb_00B0
raw_diff: added struct CSSCursorData near mnCharSel_8025C020
raw_diff: mnCharSel_8025DB34 added with PAD_STACK(0x40), name display, costume duplicate check, 1P/VS mode branches, p_kind switch
text_corpus: bot report lists mncharsel matches and improvements

key_files.role: Public mncharsel header adjusted; exact declarations not visible in loaded diff excerpt.
Static/internal mncharsel header adjusted; exact additions not visible in loaded diff excerpt.
Menu type definitions adjusted; exact field/type changes not visible in loaded diff excerpt.

what_changed.text: Expanded mncharsel.c substantially, with 2821 added and 29 deleted lines across 19 hunks.
Added or exposed local typed structure CSSCursorData with HSD_GObj pointer, byte/halfword slots, and float fields.

Added mnCharSel_8025DB34 implementation covering name display, costume duplicate selection, 1P mode animation updates, VS door/p_kind transitions, handicap/CPU slider handling, and status/icon animation paths.
Adjusted mnCharSel_8025D5AC enough for the bot report to mark it as a new 100% match, including temporary locals new_var/new_var2 and a no-op-looking conditional in the excerpt.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists mnCharSel_8025D5AC +5 bytes, 99.62% to 100.00%

what_changed.text: Bot report recorded 3 new matches: fn_80262F44, mnCharSel_8025EE8C, and mnCharSel_8025D5AC.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: issue_comment decomp-dev[bot] report, 3 new matches

what_changed.text: Bot report recorded progress in 10 unmatched items, including mnCharSel_CursorThink, mnCharSel_8025DB34, fn_802633B0, fn_8025F0E0, fn_80262648, mnCharSel_8025FB50, mnCharSel_8025FDEC, .sdata2, .sbss, and .sdata.

smart_moves.text: Used real headers/includes for newly referenced subsystems rather than source-local undeclared prototypes, including baselib, gm, and lb headers.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added include list in mncharsel.c

smart_moves.text: Used field access through existing mnCharSel global state and gmMainLib data in visible logic instead of raw pointer arithmetic in the excerpted mnCharSel_8025DB34 body.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mnCharSel_803F0DFC.doors/tags fields and mnCharSel_804D6CB0->data.data.players fields

smart_moves.text: Kept a large p_kind dispatch as a C switch, which is easier to review than preserving a generated branch ladder.

decomp_lessons.text: For large menu-state functions, preserving typed state access through known globals and structs can make generated-looking logic more reviewable while still advancing match scores.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mnCharSel_8025DB34 accesses doors, tags, icons, gmMainLib, and player fields

decomp_lessons.text: Large partial implementations can still be valuable when the report clearly separates fully matched functions from improved-but-unmatched functions.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: 3 new matches and 10 improvements in unmatched items

decomp_lessons.text: When a pragma or PAD_STACK is present in matching code, it should be treated as a narrow matching tactic rather than a style precedent unless verifier evidence explains why it is required.

naming_conventions.text: Unresolved or partially understood symbols kept address-style names such as mnCharSel_8025DB34, fn_80262F44, and fn_802633B0.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report item names fn_80262F44 and fn_802633B0

naming_conventions.text: Local variables mix semantic role names where apparent, such as sel_icon, hud_idx, costume_var, num_doors, found, hval, and hval2, with stack/register-derived names such as sp90 and temp_f31 where roles remain less refined.
Temporary match-steering names new_var and new_var2 appear in mnCharSel_8025D5AC; these are evidence of local matching work, not a recommended naming convention.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Loaded evidence does not include a build/objdiff rationale for this stack padding.

evidence_refs: raw_diff: #pragma dont_inline on before mnCharSel_8025DAA0 and off after
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Loaded evidence does not include adjacent-function regression evidence for this pragma.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Assignment inside call argument used for lb_80011E24 in mnCharSel_8025D5AC: new_var2 = &sp54.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Likely a codegen steering tactic; not enough evidence to generalize.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: No-op-looking conditional inserted after an animation request in mnCharSel_8025D5AC.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Should not be promoted as accepted style without explicit verifier evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Switch used for p_kind handling in mnCharSel_8025DB34, consistent with checking canonical C control-flow forms for branch dispatch.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Separate fully matched wins from broad partial progress in large decomp PRs
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2370 shows a large mncharsel source expansion where the bot report distinguishes 3 new 100% matches from 10 improved but still-unmatched items. Intake records should preserve that distinction instead of treating all changed functions as matched.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists 3 new matches and 10 improvements

accepted_candidate_records.title: Treat visible PAD_STACK, pragma, and no-op constructs as tactics requiring evidence
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2370 contains PAD_STACK, a scoped dont_inline pragma, assignment-in-argument steering, and a no-op-looking conditional. These are searchable matching tactics from the PR, but the loaded evidence does not justify them as general source style.

source_update_candidates.title: mncharsel path touched by large character-select decomp PR
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2370 changed src/melee/mn/mncharsel.c, src/melee/mn/mncharsel.h, src/melee/mn/mncharsel.static.h, and src/melee/mn/types.h, with the bot report scoped to main/melee/mn/mncharsel.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path-fact update for curator review; intake does not promote path ownership.

source_update_candidates.title: Require explicit rationale for no-op-looking source constructs used for matching
source_update_candidates.text: The diff shows a no-op-looking empty conditional in mnCharSel_8025D5AC alongside a reported match. Existing standards already cover matching tactics needing evidence; curator may decide whether examples like empty conditionals should be called out explicitly.
source_update_candidates.reason: Possible standards clarification only; loaded PR evidence does not establish general acceptance.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that PAD_STACK or dont_inline were accepted as general style from this PR alone.
Do not mark mnCharSel_8025DB34 or other improved functions as fully matched unless the bot report lists them as new matches.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale; no human review comments are loaded.
Do not promote header/type details beyond changed-file counts because exact hunks are not visible in the loaded excerpt.
